raw_response: {"response":"Trust the workflow as a strong base, with two required corrections before handoff: make test event results an explicit evidence item and change the remediation action from \"Send next fix\" to applying or recording the next setup fix.","next_action":"Keep the staged diagnostic flow, then update the work units and panel sections to include test event result/status, retain endpoint status, retry outcome, request ID, and error cause as diagnostic evidence, and make the completion state capture the chosen fix plus rerun result.","questions":[],"handoff":"Platform engineer should apply or record the selected setup fix and rerun the test event because the current fixture implies the right evidence path but under-specifies the test result and uses an awkward send-next-fix action.","rationale":"The fixture matches the source activity on endpoint status, retry result, request ID, and next-fix decision, and exposing API endpoint/request ID is appropriate in a setup/debugging context. It should not be accepted unchanged because the source explicitly names test event results as a decision input, while the fixture only implies them through an action and completion state; the next-fix action also reads like messaging rather than remediation."}

parsed.response: Trust the workflow as a strong base, with two required corrections before handoff: make test event results an explicit evidence item and change the remediation action from "Send next fix" to applying or recording the next setup fix.
parsed.next_action: Keep the staged diagnostic flow, then update the work units and panel sections to include test event result/status, retain endpoint status, retry outcome, request ID, and error cause as diagnostic evidence, and make the completion state capture the chosen fix plus rerun result.
parsed.handoff: Platform engineer should apply or record the selected setup fix and rerun the test event because the current fixture implies the right evidence path but under-specifies the test result and uses an awkward send-next-fix action.
parsed.rationale: The fixture matches the source activity on endpoint status, retry result, request ID, and next-fix decision, and exposing API endpoint/request ID is appropriate in a setup/debugging context. It should not be accepted unchanged because the source explicitly names test event results as a decision input, while the fixture only implies them through an action and completion state; the next-fix action also reads like messaging rather than remediation.

execution.command_display: codex exec --model gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="xhigh" --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral --color never --output-schema <schema> --output-last-message <output> -
